nba_average_free_throw_percentage_is_7: NBA average free throw percentage is ~77%
soccer_penalty_kick_conversion_rate_is: Soccer penalty kick conversion rate is ~76%
mlb_average_batting_average_is_250_hit: MLB average batting average is ~.250 (hit 1 in 4)
nfl_field_goal_success_rate_is_84: NFL field goal success rate is ~84%
pro_tennis_first_serve_success_rate_is: Pro tennis first serve success rate is ~62%
explore_probability_through_experiment: Explore probability through experiments. Run trials and watch observed frequencies converge to expected values.
p_h_p_t_0_5_law_of_large_numbers_as_tr: P(H) = P(T) = 0.5. Law of large numbers: as trials grow, the proportion of heads converges to 0.5. After 1,000 flips you’ll be within ~3% of 50%, but in any short streak anything is possible.
dice_uniform_1_n_each_d4_d6_d8_d10_d12: Dice — uniform 1/N each (d4 / d6 / d8 / d10 / d12 / d20)
pick_any_die_size_each_face_equally_li: Pick any die size. Each face equally likely: P = 1/N. The math is identical across die types — what changes is the denominator. d4 is 1/4, d20 is 1/20.
roll_two_dice_of_the_chosen_type_and_a: Roll TWO dice of the chosen type and add. The sum is no longer uniform: middle sums are MUCH more common. For 2d6, P(7)=6/36, P(2)=1/36. This is why orange properties in Monopoly get landed on the most — 6, 7, 8 are the most common sums after rolling from Jail.
equal_area_sectors_equal_probability_u: Equal-area sectors = equal probability. Unequal sectors → weighted draws. Spinners are the gentlest path into discrete distributions for elementary students.
free_throw_75_nba_3_point_36_mlb_hit_2: Free-throw 75%, NBA 3-point 36%, MLB hit ~25%. Probability isn’t always 50/50 — the math handles unequal weights the same way, just with different denominators.
marble_bag_with_vs_without_replacement: Marble Bag — with vs without replacement
with_replacement_independent_draws_wit: With replacement: independent draws. Without: probabilities CHANGE each pull — conditional probability. The exact mechanism behind hypergeometric distribution and card-game odds.
custom_design_your_own_outcome_set: Custom — design your own outcome set
build_any_discrete_distribution_test_t: Build any discrete distribution. Test the law of large numbers with skewed odds, demonstrate that simulations can answer ANY closed-form question if you have enough trials. Monte Carlo in miniature.
multiply_along_branches_add_across_lea: Multiply along branches; add across leaves. Two coin flips: HH HT TH TT each 0.25. Tree diagrams scale up to medical-test base-rate problems and Bayes’ theorem.
throw_darts_at_a_unit_square_count_how: Throw darts at a unit square; count how many land inside the inscribed quarter circle. π ≈ 4 × (inside / total). Convergence is O(1/√N) — each digit costs 100× more darts.
in_a_room_of_23_the_probability_of_a_s: In a room of 23, the probability of a shared birthday is > 50%. By 70 it’s > 99.9%. Counterintuitive because we count comparisons (23 choose 2 = 253), not people. The cleanest classroom counter to gut-feel probability.
without_replacement_each_marble_drawn_: 💡 Without replacement: Each marble drawn is removed from the bag. Probabilities change after each draw! Bag refills when empty.
keyframes_mbshake_0_100_transform_rota: @keyframes mbShake { 0%,100% { transform: rotate(0deg); } 15% { transform: rotate(-8deg); } 30% { transform: rotate(8deg); } 45% { transform: rotate(-5deg); } 60% { transform: rotate(5deg); } 75% { transform: rotate(-2deg); } }
define_each_event_as_a_fraction_e_g_1_: 🎯 Define each event as a fraction — e.g., "1 out of 20 times"
add_colored_marbles_to_a_bag_probabili: 🎱 Add colored marbles to a bag. Probability = your marble count ÷ total marbles.
drag_sliders_to_set_exact_probability_: 🎨 Drag sliders to set exact probability percentages for each outcome.
behind_one_door_a_prize_behind_two_goa: Behind one door: a prize. Behind two: goats. Pick a door, the host opens a goat-door, then you choose: stay or switch?
last_20_manual_stay_plays_green_wins_d: Last 20 manual Stay plays — green wins, dark red losses
last_20_manual_switch_plays_green_wins: Last 20 manual Switch plays — green wins, dark red losses
outcome_strips_show_your_last_20_manua: Outcome strips show your last 20 manual plays — green = win, red = loss. Visible streaks tell you what randomness actually feels like.
math_says_stay_wins_1_3_33_switch_wins: Math says: Stay wins ≈ 1/3 (33%). Switch wins ≈ 2/3 (67%). With enough trials, the math wins out.
drop_balls_through_a_peg_grid_each_peg: Drop balls through a peg grid. Each peg deflects 50/50 left or right. After enough balls, the histogram becomes a bell curve — the Central Limit Theorem in action.
gold_dashed_line_theoretical_normal_cu: ⬆ Gold dashed line: theoretical normal curve. Your empirical bars should hug it more closely with more drops.
in_a_room_of_just_23_people_there_s_a_: In a room of just 23 people, there’s a >50% chance two share a birthday. Drag the slider to explore!
run_100_simulations_of_this_room_size: Run 100 simulations of this room size
each_has_a_random_birthday_same_color_: Each 🧑 has a random birthday. Same color + glow = matching pair. Resample to see how often matches appear.
multiply_the_two_probabilities_to_get_: 💡 Multiply the two probabilities to get the joint probability. These events are independent, so P(A∩B) = P(A) × P(B).
as_you_run_more_trials_the_observed_ba: 💡 As you run more trials, the observed bars should get closer to the theoretical bars — that's the Law of Large Numbers in action!
10k_points_needed_for_2_decimal_places: ~10k points needed for 2 decimal places of π
get_5_results_in_a_row_any_mode: Get 5+ results in a row (any mode)
60_dice_rolls_all_within_10_of_expecte: 60+ dice rolls, all within 10% of expected
set_n_23_in_birthday_mode_to_see_the_p: Set n=23 in Birthday mode to see the paradox
all_outcomes_nearly_equal_in_probabili: All outcomes nearly equal in probability.
middle_outcome_dominates_approaches_no: Middle outcome dominates. Approaches normal-like shape with more buckets.
one_tail_much_heavier_than_the_other_a: One tail much heavier than the other. Asymmetric distribution.
two_outcomes_share_roughly_equal_proba: Two outcomes share roughly equal probability with the third much lower or higher.
three_sliders_set_the_relative_probabi: Three sliders set the relative probabilities of low / mid / high outcomes. The distribution shape is classified into one of four discrete shapes. No score, no reveal — sweep and notice.
hypothesis_free_text_what_combination_: Hypothesis (free text): What combination produces uniform? What about peaked?
hold_two_sliders_steady_move_one_watch: Hold two sliders steady. Move one. Watch the shape.
find_two_settings_that_produce_the_sam: Find two settings that produce the same shape.
what_raw_weights_produce_a_peaked_shap: What raw weights produce a peaked shape?
i_understand_explain_in_my_own_words: I understand — explain in my own words
explain_how_relative_weights_produce_s: Explain how relative weights produce shape.
design_note_discrete_4_shape_classific: Design note: discrete 4-shape classification; no goodness-of-fit score; no reveal — by design.
